biography: Mikael, representing Kurk Golf, etched a concise yet distinct chapter into the annals of the Hector Trophée through his singular participation in this challenging pairs competition. His presence marked a specific moment in the tournament's evolving narrative.

During his dedicated appearance on the Hector Trophée fairways, Mikael engaged with the rigorous demands of both the individual and pair formats. While neither the coveted Hector Trophée for his partnership nor the prestigious individual Victor Trophy ultimately adorned his collection, his committed effort and presence contributed to the competitive spirit of that edition.

As the Hector Trophée 2026 looms, Mikael will, as anticipated, not be among the returning competitors. His journey in Hector events is now considered concluded, marking the retirement of a player whose brief yet memorable contribution forms a part of the tournament's rich history.
